source.multipart_basis: Decomposition inferred from the original prompt; no rulebook citations added beyond those carried by the original sample.

full_prompt: Design the chassis + side-panel + wheel + guide structure of a model-solar-car benchmark as a multi-part assembly. Regulatory constraints: vehicle must present a cross-section of at least 150 cm^2 to the oncoming airflow on the drive direction; rigid side panels must be at least 100 mm long x 50 mm high; the vehicle interface to the PV array uses a pair of 4 mm banana sockets separated by 18 mm on centerline; vehicle is constrained to a 0.5 mm stainless steel guide wire through a front guide eye and a rear guide slot; total wheelbase between 180 mm and 260 mm. The assembly consists of the main chassis deck, two rigid side panels (left + right), a front guide-eye bracket, a rear guide-slot bracket, four wheels, a PV banana-socket plate, and the fastener / bond interfaces. Material: ABS 3D printed or CNC-machined sheet (Young's modulus 2.3 GPa, Poisson 0.35, tensile yield 40 MPa, ultimate 42 MPa, density 1050 kg/m^3). PV array supplied: 6-cell monocrystalline silicon panel of mass 65 g mounted on the top chassis deck spanning at least 180 x 100 mm. Load cases: (LC1) 5x g static factor on total car mass of target 200 g at rest; (LC2) guide-wire lateral pull: 1.5 N at the front guide eye simulating wire drag/deflection; (LC3) PV cantilever: array mass 65 g with 3 g vertical bump applied at the array centroid while the four wheel contact patches are fully fixed; (LC4) 5 minute endurance at 2 Hz cyclic loading at 5% of LC1 representing track vibration for the standard 20 m class A run, 600 cycles. Pass/fail: (R1) max von Mises below 26 MPa (yield 40 / 1.5) across LC1-LC3, (R2) chassis deflection under array loaded under LC3 below 1.5 mm, (R3) cross-section rule 150 cm^2 and side-panel rules verified geometrically, (R4) total mass below 300 g, (R5) first torsional chassis mode above 40 Hz. Verification is performed on the assembled multi-body model with bolted / bonded interfaces; per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail. Deliverables: STEP AP242 with all bodies, FEA per case on assembly, modal, and a geometric-compliance check block.
short_prompt:
Design the chassis + side-panel + wheel + guide structure of a model solar car benchmark as a multi-part assembly.

Regulatory constraints: oncoming cross-section >= 150 cm^2, rigid side panels >= 100 mm long x 50 mm high, PV interface is a pair of 4 mm banana sockets at 18 mm centerline, guide is a 0.5 mm stainless wire through a front guide eye and rear guide slot, wheelbase 180-260 mm. Chassis material ABS (E 2.3 GPa, Poisson 0.35, yield 40 MPa, ultimate 42 MPa, density 1050 kg/m^3). PV array supplied: 6-cell monocrystalline panel of mass 65 g spanning at least 180 x 100 mm.

Load cases.
- LC1 5 g static factor on a 200 g target mass at rest.
- LC2 guide-wire lateral pull 1.5 N at the front guide eye.
- LC3 PV cantilever: 65 g array mass with 3 g vertical bump at array centroid.
- LC4 endurance: 2 Hz cyclic at 5% of LC1 for 600 cycles over a 20 m class A run.

Total mass below 300 g; first torsional mode above 40 Hz.

prompt.title: Model solar car chassis (multi-part assembly)
prompt.brief: ABS model solar car: main chassis deck + left/right side panels + front guide-eye + rear guide-slot + 4 wheels + banana-socket PV plate, on a 0.5 mm guide wire.

parts.name: chassis_deck
parts.role: main ABS deck supporting PV plate and tying side panels / guides together

parts.name: side_panel
parts.role: rigid left / right side panel satisfying the 100x50 mm rigidity rule and cross-section rule

parts.name: front_guide_eye_bracket
parts.role: bracket carrying the guide eye that rides the 0.5 mm stainless steel wire at the front

parts.name: rear_guide_slot_bracket
parts.role: bracket carrying the rear guide slot that captures the 0.5 mm stainless steel wire

parts.name: wheel
parts.role: road wheel pressed onto axle; four total

parts.name: pv_banana_socket_plate
parts.role: plate holding the 4 mm banana sockets at 18 mm centerline separation

assembly_envelope.overall_bbox: team-selected within the 150 cm^2 cross-section rule, wheelbase 180-260 mm
assembly_envelope.coordinate_system: X along drive direction, Y transverse, Z up

boundary_conditions.interfaces: side panels bonded / bolted to chassis deck; guide-eye and guide-slot brackets bolted to chassis; banana-socket plate bonded into deck; wheels on axles pinned to chassis
boundary_conditions.support: four wheel contact patches fixed in LC3

prompt.objective: minimize total mass within 300 g while satisfying geometric rules and modal floor

interfaces.load_path: guide wire pull into chassis via front bracket

fem_check_policy.rationale: Multipart entries are verified on the assembled model. Per-part stress is reported but does not gate pass/fail.

notes.why_multi_part: The car explicitly has a chassis deck, two rigid side panels, front and rear wire-guide brackets, four wheels, and a PV socket plate as distinct parts joined by bolts or bonded interfaces.
notes.speculation_caveats: Part counts and interface types are speculative; materials, loads, and thresholds inherit the original sample verbatim.
